reasoning: Under a Trump presidency, proposed policies such as broad baseline tariffs and stricter immigration enforcement are widely anticipated by economists to exert upward pressure on prices. Additionally, with inflation already hovering around 2.5% in late 2024 and sticky service-sector inflation persisting, the baseline path to a sub-2.5% inflation rate in 2025 is narrow. Trump's policies—especially tariffs which can be implemented quickly via executive action—would likely cause a one-time price level increase, preventing the inflation rate from dropping and staying below the 2.5% threshold for the year.
